events.content: Assigned to review the relayfile-cloud workspace.ts refactor and handle relayfile-cloud git ops after review passes. Preparing the target repo baseline now so I can move quickly when the implementation lands.

Stage @relayfile/core source/config and the root workspace manifest, but exclude generated dist output from the commit: Stage @relayfile/core source/config and the root workspace manifest, but exclude generated dist output from the commit

raw.question: Stage @relayfile/core source/config and the root workspace manifest, but exclude generated dist output from the commit
raw.chosen: Stage @relayfile/core source/config and the root workspace manifest, but exclude generated dist output from the commit
raw.reasoning: The repo-level .gitignore excludes dist/, existing packages do not track built artifacts, and the durable source of truth for the new package is its src/ plus package metadata. Committing generated output here would diverge from the repository's existing package conventions.

events.content: Committed and pushed the new @relayfile/core package on feat/conformance-suite-and-skill, then updated the existing relayfile PR to describe the extracted core package and its 8 business-logic modules.

raw.adjustments: Reused the branch's existing PR instead of opening a duplicate, and expanded its title/body to cover both the earlier branch work and the new core package handoff.

events.content: Agent-4 has landed the DO-backed StorageAdapter bridge in relayfile-cloud workspace.ts and started moving read-only surfaces to @relayfile/core. Review focus will be the bridge boundary, residual inline business logic, and write-side state transitions once the refactor lands.

raw.question: Record the package directory rename as a follow-up commit on the existing relayfile PR instead of amending the earlier core-package commit
raw.chosen: Record the package directory rename as a follow-up commit on the existing relayfile PR instead of amending the earlier core-package commit
raw.reasoning: The coordinator changed the repo layout after the initial PR update. A separate commit preserves a clear branch history, avoids rewriting the pushed commit, and cleanly isolates the mechanical path rename from the functional package introduction.

events.content: Followed the repo-wide rename from packages/relayfile-core to packages/core with a separate pushed commit and updated the existing relayfile PR text to match the new path.

raw.adjustments: Excluded unrelated package rename work from the commit and propagated the new path into the PR body so downstream relayfile-cloud integration can reference the correct local dependency target.

events.content: Committed the follow-up package-dir rename from packages/relayfile to packages/cli and packages/relayfile-sdk to packages/sdk, preserving it as a separate pushed commit on the existing relayfile PR.

raw.adjustments: Limited the commit to the mechanical path and metadata updates so downstream relayfile-cloud work can follow the new local dependency and workspace paths without mixing in unrelated repo edits.
